message:
feat(wa): add top-level 'wa approve' command for human approval flow

Extract the approval evaluation logic from the robot-only code path
into a shared evaluate_approve() function, and expose a new top-level
'wa approve' CLI command so that human operators can submit approval
codes directly without going through the robot subcommand.

CLI addition (crates/wa/src/main.rs):
- Add Commands::Approve variant with code, --pane, --fingerprint, and
  --dry-run arguments matching the robot approve interface
- Wire the new command into the main run() dispatch, opening the
  storage handle and calling evaluate_approve() with actor_kind="human"

Refactor:
- Extract evaluate_approve() from evaluate_robot_approve(), adding an
  actor_kind parameter that flows into the audit record's actor_kind
  field and decision_reason message
- evaluate_robot_approve() now delegates to evaluate_approve() with
  actor_kind="robot", preserving backward compatibility
- Audit records now show "Human submitted approval code" vs "Robot
  submitted approval code" based on the entry point
- Input summary uses "wa approve" instead of "wa robot approve" for
  the human path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
